name: Fig Pastries
recipeInstructions:
2 Eggs
1 cup Brown sugar; firmly packed
1/2 cup Orange juice
3 tbsp Lemon juice
1 tsp Grated lemon peel
1 tsp Vanilla
2 cups California dried figs (remove stems)
1 cup Pitted dates
1 cup Walnuts
In a bowl, combine flour, sugar, and cornstarch for the pastry. Add butter and process or mix until the dough holds together. Divide into thirds. Roll out two-thirds of dough on a baking sheet (13-inch x 17-inch) to reach all corners evenly. Prick with fork. Bake at 350°F for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, roll out remaining dough between sheets of plastic wrap or waxed paper to a rectangle about half the size (9-inch x 13-inch) of the baking sheet. Cut into long strips about 1/4 inch wide. In another bowl, combine eggs, brown sugar, juices, lemon peel, and vanilla. Blend well. Add figs, dates, and nuts; if using a mixer, finely chop first. Process until evenly minced. Spread filling over baked pastry; top with strips of dough, crisscrossing diagonally. Return to oven and bake for 30 minutes, until pastry strips are lightly browned. Cool. Cut into small bars.
